other_observers: Brian Gibbons, Steve Brown, Joe Roller, John Drummond and a few others; I was the first to find the bird in the net, but it is most likely the bird seen the day before by Brian Gibbons.
duration: in net 2 minutes, in hand 3 minutes
specific_location: RMBO Banding Station, Chico Basin Ranch
description: This bird was small with vireo-like bill, small hook, and legs, strong and stouter than a warbler. It had a medium gray crown blending into a greenish gray back. A dark eye was surrounded by a white broad ring, through which a dark eyestripe ran, in the front forming a distinct dark lore.   The throat was noticeably yellow, fading on the belly to a creamy color that had some yellow tints. The undertail coverts again became noticeably yellow.   The wings were without wingbars, the tail was short and the over appearance was of a chubby, small vireo.Behaviors: I found the bird in a net where I assumed it would be one of the previous day's Tennessee Warblers, but this bird had distinct vireo features and not those of a warbler.   In the net, the bird did very little.Call: none heardPlumage: alternate
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
